secondary_archetype: AI-focused Design Enablement / Internal Tools Builder

level_signals: 5+ years of experience in design thinking, product design, user experience, design engineering, or program management
Track record of coaching, teaching, or enabling others
Expected to operate independently and drive org-level transformation

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of experience in design thinking, product design, user experience, design engineering, or program management, and experience partnering with design teams.

Demonstrated, hands-on experience building AI-powered tools, agents, automations, or workflows that transformed real work processes (not just using AI as a chatbot — building with it) for designers, researchers, creative teams, or product teams.
Track record of coaching, teaching, or enabling others — formally or informally — with evidence that people you've helped actually changed how they work.
Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to explain technical concepts to non-technical audiences and adapt your style to different learners.
Comfort working across multiple workstreams and relationships simultaneously (you'll be supporting Design teams at varying stages of their AI journey).
"Product builder" mindset to solve the "last mile" problem: transforming standalone AI experiments into polished, production-ready products that prioritize discoverability and ease of use.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience with design and research tooling ecosystems (Figma plugins, design system management, plugin APIs).
Proficiency with AI development tools and platforms (e.g., Claude, Claude Code, Codex, custom agent frameworks, API integrations, workflow automation tools).
Experience with change management, organizational transformation, or large-scale enablement programs.
Experience building and scaling internal tools, templates, or playbooks that were adopted beyond your immediate team.

scope.surface: AI-assisted tooling and workflows for Stripe's Design organization (product, brand, marketing designers, researchers, content designers, design engineers)
scope.business_model: internal platform / enablement role at a B2B fintech company
primary_metrics_inferred: Proportion of Design Stripes defaulting to AI in their work
Individual and cohort progress against a defined maturity model
core_responsibilities: Identify and document the highest-leverage workflow transformations within Design's day-to-day work
Partner with designers to imagine AI-powered tools and agents that accelerate velocity
Build custom tools, agents, automations, and skills tailored to Design's workflows
Enable and uplevel designers to build and iterate on their own tools over time
Recognize patterns across Design and systematically scale what works
Document every tool, playbook, and transformation pattern built
Track individual and cohort progress against a defined maturity model
Prepare Designers for an agentic future including multi-agent workflows
Ensure all tooling meets Stripe's quality, security, and performance expectations
Source and evaluate vendors; interface with legal, security, and procurement teams

green_flags: Clearly articulated mission and success metrics (workflow transformation, maturity model progression)
High-impact, novel role building a new team and operating model — significant ownership opportunity
Explicit focus on building real things ('build in real time alongside the people you support') rather than slide decks
Cross-functional exposure to design, engineering, research, product, legal, security, and procurement
red_flags: No location or remote/hybrid policy stated — candidates cannot assess relocation requirements
No visa sponsorship or work authorization guidance provided
Role is explicitly described as 'a new team building a new operating model' — high ambiguity and potential for scope instability
Posting and last-updated dates not available — unclear how current this listing is

ambiguities: The JD does not specify a location, office requirement, or remote eligibility. Location.type is set to 'unclear'.

The role title is 'Design Program Manager' but the responsibilities are heavily technical (building agents, automations, custom tools) — could be classified as Technical Program Manager or a hybrid Technical/Program Manager archetype. Classified as Technical Program Manager with secondary note.

Minimum requirements include 'experience in design thinking, product design, user experience, design engineering, OR program management' — very broad; it is unclear which domain is truly required vs. acceptable alternative.
